prevalence.source: WHO/CDC herpes zoster epidemiology context; regional adult health surveys
prevalence.trend: increasing with aging population and immunosuppression prevalence

incidence.source: Herpes zoster cohort studies and vaccine era surveillance

burden.dalyPerCapita: low overall; post-herpetic neuralgia drives disability

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Typical dermatomal vesicular rash with neuropathic pain; PCR from lesions for atypical cases; assess ocular involvement urgently

publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Hundreds of thousands to >1 million episodes per year (age-dependent)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; early antiviral treatment reduces complications

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care/UPA; referral for ophthalmic or disseminated cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acyclovir/valacyclovir within 72 hours, analgesia, neuropathic pain management; vaccine access mainly private
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited access to zoster vaccination in SUS, delayed presentation in some regions
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Effective antivirals widely available; clinical recognition is straightforward
